name: Livestock Tavern
tagline: Chinatown's rustic-chic dinner spot — seasonal farm-to-table dishes, killer cocktails, and a vibe that straddles casual and refined.
mustOrder: seasonal pasta — changes with what's local
craft cocktails — the bar program is legit
parking: Street parking on Bethel St; nearby paid lots
talkStory: Opened in 2013 by chef Jesse Cruz and Dusty Grable in a former Chinatown warehouse, helping ignite the neighborhood's dining renaissance. Cruz, a Culinary Institute of America grad, changes the menu weekly based on what local farms deliver. Won multiple Hale 'Aina awards for Best New Restaurant and Best Farm-to-Table. The bone marrow appetizer has been on since day one because regulars refuse to let it go. The cocktail program uses house-made syrups and local citrus.
